title: Non-Amazon Scrape Thread: LiverpoolStyle Through Shapellx

metadata.purpose: Focused transcript for a Codex non-Amazon scrape thread that refreshed multiple retailer review-image datasets, patched scrapers for rate-limited catalog discovery, rebuilt aggregate reports, and prepared git/S3/transcript closeout.
metadata.scope_note: This is a focused handoff transcript because a full Codex desktop export was not available from inside the repository.

messages.text: Asked Codex to do the liverpoolstyle.com full catalog scrape, then questioned why there were so few rows and asked what could be done to get the needed data.

Scraped LiverpoolStyle using the public Klaviyo review/image surface. The full scrape completed with 10 rows and 2 qualified rows. Codex added or updated `data-pipelines/non-amazon/scripts/step_1_raw_scrape/scrape_liverpoolstyle_reviews.py`.
Repeatedly asked for the next scrape and approved proceeding, while reminding Codex to avoid redundant work because concurrent Codex chats were scraping other sites.
Checked active scrape claim files and the aggregate report before each new target. Avoided sites that were already claimed, recently refreshed, blocked, or completed in other chats.

Refreshed Evelyn Bobbie. Patched `scrape_evelynbobbie_reviews.py` to use Yotpo v3 media-first review retrieval, broaden deterministic bra-size parsing, and support cached product fallback. Final run produced 2 rows and 1 qualified row.
Refreshed Wool& successfully. Final output produced 4,426 image rows and roughly 612 qualified rows. No code patch was needed in this thread for Wool&.

Refreshed Miss Me. The live Shopify catalog returned 429, so Codex patched `scrape_missme_reviews.py` to fall back to the prior product list while refreshing live Okendo review/image endpoints. Final output produced 293 rows and 196 qualified rows.
Refreshed Wildfang. The live catalog returned 429, so Codex patched `scrape_wildfang_reviews.py` with cached product-list fallback. Final output produced 384 rows and 155 qualified rows.
Refreshed MBM Swim with the existing scraper. Final output produced 617 rows and 99 qualified rows.
Recommended Harper Wilde as the next target because it was unclaimed, had a dedicated scraper, and had older high-yield output. User approved.

Claimed Harper Wilde, patched `scrape_harper_wilde_reviews.py` to honor `FWM_DATA_DIR`, write current intake-schema output files, include intake context columns, and fall back to cached product URLs when the sitemap returns 429. The refresh scanned 315 cached products and wrote 65 rows with 30 qualified rows. Codex noted the lower row count was because the current product-widget path deduped aggregate-style repeated images.

Recommended Kut from the Kloth next. User approved. Refreshed Kut from the Kloth with the dedicated Okendo scraper. Final output scanned 444 products, wrote 261 rows, and yielded 87 qualified rows in the aggregate report.

Recommended Shapellx next. User approved. The first live products.json call returned 429, so Codex patched `scrape_shapellx_reviews.py` to use a cached previous summary product-list fallback. An uncapped full refresh timed out; a tiny probe overwrote the output with a 1-product file, then Codex immediately restored a full-catalog bounded refresh using `--limit-pages-per-product 4`. Final Shapellx output scanned 186 products, wrote 1,507 rows, and yielded 121 qualified rows.

For each completed scrape, Codex updated the corresponding active claim file in `FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/_active_scrape_claims`, reran `summarize_non_amazon_scrapes.py`, and verified the updated row in `FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/reports/non_amazon_merchant_scrape_summary.csv`.
Said the chat thread is done and asked Codex to update git, S3, and the transcripts table.
Prepared this focused transcript JSON for upload to the `codex_chat_transcripts` Supabase table, and began closeout steps for scoped git commit/push and S3 sync.
